The fact that the checking account balance is negative because negative balances increase the likelihood of being classified as a bad credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[1]
The fact that the loan duration is longer because extended repayment periods typically expose lenders to greater uncertainty, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk.[2]
The fact that there is no checking account because the absence of a checking account does not increase credit risk and may correlate with more favorable credit outcomes, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[3]
